name: Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Circular Route
name_original: Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging in Serra De Collserola offers an extensive natural environment with diverse terrain, making it a prime destination for outdoor enthusiasts near Barcelona. This metropolitan park encompasses over 8,000 hectares, characterized by rugged mountains, valleys, and watercourses. The landscape features mixed forests of white pine and holm oak, alongside scrubland, providing varied running surfaces and scenic backdrops. Best jogging routes in Serra De Collserola The most popular jogging route is Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Circular Route, a 12.7 miles (20.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Budellera Spring Trail, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route leads through natural landscapes, passing the charming Budellera fountain. Local runners also love the Carretera de les Aigües – Cypresses Viewpoint loop from Funicular Plaça Doctor Andreu, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail leading through a popular, relatively flat dirt road with panoramic views of Barcelona, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Jogging in Serra De Collserola is defined by rugged mountains, mixed forests, and numerous panoramic viewpoints. The extensive network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation gains. The routes in Serra De Collserola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 6000 runners have used komoot to explore Serra De Collserola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Serra de Collserola?
A: Serra de Collserola offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 80 trails available on komoot. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Serra de Collserola?
A: The park features diverse terrain, ranging from relatively flat, unpaved dirt roads like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33904297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de les Aigües&lt;/a&gt; to hilly trails with significant elevation gains. You'll encounter mixed forests of white pine and holm oak, as well as scrubland, providing varied running surfaces and scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Serra de Collserola has several easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964415222/budellera-spring-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Budellera Spring Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0-mile (4.8 km) path that leads through natural landscapes, passing the charming Budellera fountain. There are 5 easy routes in total, perfect for a gentle run.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while running in Serra de Collserola?
A: Many routes offer impressive panoramic vistas of Barcelona and the surrounding areas. You can find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Medir Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; and the Santa Creu d'Olorda Hermitage are also accessible along some trails.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the running routes in Serra de Collserola?
A: Yes, the park features several beautiful waterfalls. You can discover natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can add a refreshing element to your run.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Serra de Collserola?
A: Serra de Collserola is generally dog-friendly, with many trails suitable for running with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local regulations and other park users.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the park?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Serra de Collserola are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963092603/santa-creu-d-olorda-mirador-dels-xiprers-sant-pere-martir-parc-collserola-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views and varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Serra de Collserola?
A: The park is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Early mornings or late afternoons are ideal during summer to avoid the midday heat, and you might even catch an enchanting sunrise or sunset.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Serra de Collserola?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners often praise the extensive natural environment, the diverse terrain, and the panoramic views of Barcelona, making it a favorite for both challenging runs and scenic jogs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Budellera fountain?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964415222/budellera-spring-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Budellera Spring Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0-mile (4.8 km) route specifically designed to lead through natural landscapes and past the charming Budellera fountain, a notable natural feature of the park.

Q: How accessible is Serra de Collserola by public transport for runners?
A: Serra de Collserola is well-connected to Barcelona, making it accessible by public transport. Many trailheads, including those for the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33904297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de les Aigües&lt;/a&gt;, can be reached via funicular or bus services from the city, allowing for easy access to your running adventure.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the park offers numerous long-distance trails with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963092603/santa-creu-d-olorda-mirador-dels-xiprers-sant-pere-martir-parc-collserola-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, at 12.7 miles (20.4 km) with over 700 meters of elevation gain, is a prime example of a difficult route popular among local runners.
tips:
It is a large forest area that connects Barcelona with the Collserola mountain range. Its 17 hectares are the result of the sum of two rural estates and the remains of a castle, La Oreneta, which gives the park its name.

It is an excursion in a forest within the city, in which the municipal intervention has improved the road network, has designed rest areas and areas for children's play, the largest with table tennis tables. Another highlight is the picnic areas, especially the one in the highest part of the park. The tables enjoy the shade of a large carob tree and you can contemplate an exceptional panoramic view of Barcelona, from Sant Adrià de Besòs to Prat de Llobregat. Next to the park are the Can Caralleu public swimming pools, ideal to complete a summer day.
A place that must be visited both for its views and for its different options on the way.

It is located at the southern end of the Collserola mountain range, on Passeig de les Aigües, below Sant Pere Mártir. It can be accessed via Passeig de les Aigües from the walkway that goes over the road from Sarriá to Vallvidrera (BV-1462) and also from Plaza Mireia, in the municipality of Esplugues de Llobregat, passing through the wooden walkway next to the Sant Pere Mártir water tank.

   From the viewpoint you can see part of the Barcelona plain, the Llobregat delta and the mountains that surround it. A few years ago, when the site was restored, some specimens of cypresses were added to the three already existing ones, which give the viewpoint its name.

In this area you have a romantic chapel, a restaurant and a picknick area.
The chapel is only open on Saturdays from 11 to 13 o'clock. The restaurant and picknick area are perfect for a stop when hiking through the Collserola park. The restaurant might seem a bit expensive, but for being in the middle of the park it's okay.
Simple and small sanctuary of bricks and tiles with the image of Santa Maria de Collserola, which from its tiny display case observes the Sacred Heart located at the top of Tibidabo.
